rules.description: Write the failing test first. Never write implementation before a failing test exists.
Verify the test actually fails before committing a FAIL commit.
Write the minimum implementation required to pass. No more.
Verify the full relevant test suite passes before committing a PASS commit.
Refactor without changing behavior. All tests must pass after refactoring.
Test happy path first.
Test structural validations second (null, empty, whitespace, default).
Test logical validations third (business rules).
Test external validations fourth (existence checks).
Test dependency validations fifth (broker-specific failures).
Test dependency exceptions sixth (storage/API errors).
Test service exceptions seventh (catch-all unexpected errors).
Structural validations must break immediately (circuit-breaking): null entity throws immediately.
Continuous validations must collect ALL invalid fields before throwing.
Continuous validations use upsertable exception data (UpsertDataList).
Continuous validations use dynamic rules: { Condition = ..., Message = ... }.
Hybrid continuous validations validate parent before child properties.
Always verify exact broker calls (Times.Once, Times.Never).
Always end with VerifyNoOtherCalls() on all mocks.
Always verify the logging broker received the expected error.
Keep validation and exception behaviors local and explicit.
Use deep cloning (DeepCloner) to prevent shared state between test objects.
Use randomized data (Tynamix.ObjectFiller). Never hard-code test values.
Use Xeption.SameExceptionAs() for exception equality comparison.
Test higher-order logic, not primitive broker details.
Only validate fields the processing service actually uses.
Test shifter operations: entity → bool, entity → count.
Test combination operations: retrieve+add (EnsureExists), retrieve+modify (Upsert).
Test exception mapping from foundation to processing exceptions.
Test multi-entity flow combinations.
Test call order when flow depends on it.
Prefer natural order over mock-sequence style verification.
Verify orchestration-level exception wrapping and unwrapping.
Do NOT test dependency call order in aggregation service tests.
Do NOT use mock-sequence style order assertions for aggregation services.
Test only basic structural validations and exposure-level aggregation behavior.
Unit-test every success code mapping.
Unit-test every error code mapping.
Unit-test auth failure mappings.
Acceptance-test every endpoint.
Clean up all test data after acceptance tests.
Emulate external resources not owned by the microservice in acceptance tests.
Core components are test-driven.
Test element existence, properties, and actions for core components.
Test styles when they are part of the component contract.
Core components integrate with exactly one view service.
Base components: test exposed APIs and wrapper behavior.
Pages generally do not require unit tests.
Mirror the partial-class split in tests: root + Logic + Validations + Exceptions.
Root test file contains setup, mocks, and helper methods.
Logic file contains happy-path tests.
Validations file contains validation failure tests.
Exceptions file contains dependency/service exception tests.
Use GWT pattern: Given / When / Then with inline comments.
Mock all dependencies using Moq.
Use FluentAssertions for readable assertions.
Use xUnit: [Fact] for single cases, [Theory][InlineData] for parameterized.
Test names: Should{Action}Async or ShouldThrow{Exception}On{Action}If{Condition}AndLogItAsync.
Foundation Add test implementation must follow the exact 13-step order.
